title.raw: 《儒林列傳》 Biographies of Confucian Scholars

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：余讀功令，至於廣厲學官之路，未嘗不廢書而嘆也。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian says: When I read the imperial statutes and came to the provisions for broadening and encouraging the paths of academic officials, I never failed to set aside my book and sigh.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: Whenever I read the imperial statutes and reach the provisions for expanding the pathways of official scholarship, I cannot help but put down my book and sigh.

sentences.zh: 曰：嗟乎！

translations.literal: He said: Alas!
translations.idiomatic: He exclaimed: Alas!

sentences.zh: 夫周室衰而關雎作，幽厲微而禮樂壞，諸侯恣行，政由彊國。

translations.literal: When the House of Zhou declined, the Guan Ju ode was composed; when Kings You and Li were diminished, ritual and music fell into ruin; the feudal lords acted willfully, and governance was dictated by the powerful states.
translations.idiomatic: As the Zhou dynasty waned, the ode "Guan Ju" was composed; as Kings You and Li grew feeble, ritual and music fell to ruin. The feudal lords acted as they pleased, and political power rested with the strongest states.

sentences.zh: 故孔子閔王路廢而邪道興，於是論次詩書，修起禮樂。

translations.literal: Therefore Confucius grieved that the royal way had been abandoned and heterodox paths had arisen, and so he edited and arranged the Odes and Documents, and restored ritual and music.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius, grieving that the Way of the kings had fallen into disuse while heterodox doctrines flourished, set about editing the Odes and the Documents and reviving the rites and music.

sentences.zh: 適齊聞韶，三月不知肉味。

translations.literal: When he visited Qi and heard the Shao music, for three months he did not notice the taste of meat.
translations.idiomatic: When he traveled to Qi and heard the Shao music performed, for three months he was so enraptured that he could not taste his food.

sentences.zh: 自衛返魯，然後樂正，雅頌各得其所。

translations.literal: After he returned to Lu from Wei, the music was then corrected, and the Ya and Song sections each found their proper place.
translations.idiomatic: Only after his return from Wei to Lu did he set the music in order, restoring the Ya and Song sections each to their proper place.

sentences.zh: 世以混濁莫能用，是以仲尼干七十餘君無所遇，曰「茍有用我者，期月而已矣」。

translations.literal: The age was so muddied and turbid that no one could employ him; thus Confucius solicited more than seventy rulers but found no acceptance, saying, "If anyone were to employ me, within a single year I could accomplish something."
translations.idiomatic: The age was so mired in chaos that none would employ him. Confucius appealed to more than seventy rulers, yet none took him in. He said, "Were anyone to employ me, within a single year I would have something to show for it."

sentences.zh: 西狩獲麟，曰「吾道窮矣」。

translations.literal: When a unicorn was captured during a hunt in the west, he said, "My Way has reached its end."
translations.idiomatic: When a qilin was captured during a hunt in the west, he said, "My Way has come to its end."

sentences.zh: 故因史記作春秋，以當王法，其辭微而指博，後世學者多錄焉。

translations.literal: Therefore, drawing on the historical records, he composed the Spring and Autumn Annals to serve as the law of a true king; its language is subtle yet its meaning far-reaching, and scholars of later generations have widely transmitted it.
translations.idiomatic: Drawing upon the historical records, he composed the Spring and Autumn Annals as a standard of kingly governance. Its language is subtle, yet its implications are vast, and scholars of later ages have widely studied and transmitted it.

sentences.zh: 自孔子卒後，七十子之徒散游諸侯，大者為師傅卿相，小者友教士大夫，或隱而不見。

translations.literal: After Confucius passed away, his seventy disciples dispersed and traveled among the feudal lords; the prominent ones became tutors, ministers, and chancellors; the lesser ones befriended and taught scholar-officials; and some went into seclusion and were not seen.
translations.idiomatic: After Confucius died, his seventy disciples scattered across the states. The most prominent among them rose to become tutors, ministers, and chancellors; the lesser ones befriended and instructed the scholar-officials; and some withdrew into seclusion, never to be seen again.

sentences.zh: 故子路居衛，子張居陳，澹臺子羽居楚，子夏居西河，子貢終於齊。

translations.literal: Thus Zilu settled in Wei, Zizhang settled in Chen, Dantai Ziyu settled in Chu, Zixia settled at the West River, and Zigong ended his days in Qi.
translations.idiomatic: And so Zilu settled in Wei, Zizhang in Chen, Dantai Ziyu in Chu, Zixia along the West River, and Zigong ended his days in Qi.

sentences.zh: 如田子方、段干木、吳起、禽滑釐之屬，皆受業於子夏之倫，為王者師。

translations.literal: Men such as Tian Zifang, Duan Ganmu, Wu Qi, and Qin Guli all studied under the circle of Zixia and became teachers of kings.
translations.idiomatic: Figures like Tian Zifang, Duan Ganmu, Wu Qi, and Qin Guli all studied under the circle of Zixia and went on to become teachers of kings.

sentences.zh: 是時獨魏文侯好學。

translations.literal: At that time, only Marquis Wen of Wei was fond of learning.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, only Marquis Wen of Wei was devoted to learning.

sentences.zh: 后陵遲以至于始皇，天下并爭於戰國，懦術既絀焉，然齊魯之閒，學者獨不廢也。

translations.literal: Afterward, things gradually declined until the time of the First Emperor; the realm was consumed by the wars of the Warring States, and Confucian learning was suppressed. Yet in the region between Qi and Lu, scholars alone did not abandon their studies.
translations.idiomatic: In the gradual decline that followed, down to the time of the First Emperor, the realm was consumed by the rivalries of the Warring States, and Confucian learning was cast aside. Yet in the lands between Qi and Lu, scholars alone kept the tradition alive.

sentences.zh: 於威、宣之際，孟子、荀卿之列，咸遵夫子之業而潤色之，以學顯於當世。

translations.literal: During the reigns of Kings Wei and Xuan of Qi, scholars of the stature of Mencius and Xunzi all followed the Master's enterprise and embellished it, becoming prominent in their age through their learning.
translations.idiomatic: During the reigns of Kings Wei and Xuan of Qi, scholars of the caliber of Mencius and Xunzi carried forward the Master's legacy and refined it, achieving renown in their own day through their scholarship.

sentences.zh: 及至秦之季世，焚詩書，阬術士，六藝從此缺焉。

translations.literal: When it came to the final age of Qin, they burned the Odes and Documents, buried scholars alive, and from this point the Six Arts were left incomplete.
translations.idiomatic: In the last days of the Qin, the Odes and the Documents were burned and scholars were buried alive. From that time onward, the Six Arts were left in ruins.

sentences.zh: 陳涉之王也，而魯諸儒持孔氏之禮器往歸陳王。

translations.literal: When Chen She declared himself king, the Confucian scholars of Lu took the ritual vessels of the Kong family and went to submit to King Chen.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen She declared himself king, the Confucian scholars of Lu took up the ritual vessels of the Kong family and went to pledge their allegiance to him.

sentences.zh: 於是孔甲為陳涉博士，卒與涉俱死。

translations.literal: Thereupon Kong Jia became an erudite under Chen She, and in the end died together with him.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Jia became one of Chen She's erudites, and in the end perished alongside him.

sentences.zh: 陳涉起匹夫，驅瓦合適戍，旬月以王楚，不滿半歲竟滅亡，其事至微淺，然而縉紳先生之徒負孔子禮器往委質為臣者，何也？

translations.literal: Chen She rose from common origins, drove together a ragtag band of conscripts, within a month proclaimed himself king of Chu, and in less than half a year was utterly destroyed; his enterprise was the most trivial and shallow. Yet why did the gentlemen scholars carry the ritual vessels of Confucius and go to pledge themselves as his subjects?
translations.idiomatic: Chen She rose from the humblest origins, rallied a ragtag band of garrison conscripts, proclaimed himself king of Chu within a month, and was utterly destroyed in less than half a year — his enterprise could not have been more trifling. Yet the robed and belted scholars carried Confucius's ritual vessels to his court and pledged their service. Why?

sentences.zh: 以秦焚其業，積怨而發憤于陳王也。

translations.literal: Because Qin had burned their works, they had accumulated resentment and vented their indignation through King Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Because Qin had burned their life's work, they had stored up such resentment that they channeled their fury through King Chen.

sentences.zh: 及高皇帝誅項籍，舉兵圍魯，魯中諸儒尚講誦習禮樂，弦歌之音不絕，豈非聖人之遺化，好禮樂之國哉？

translations.literal: When the High Emperor destroyed Xiang Ji and raised troops to besiege Lu, the Confucian scholars within Lu were still lecturing, reciting, and practicing ritual and music, and the sounds of strings and singing did not cease — was this not the lingering influence of the sage, a land devoted to ritual and music?
translations.idiomatic: When the High Emperor defeated Xiang Yu and his armies laid siege to Lu, the Confucian scholars within the city were still lecturing, reciting, and practicing the rites and music; the sounds of strings and song never ceased. Was this not the enduring legacy of the sage — a land truly devoted to ritual and music?

sentences.zh: 故孔子在陳曰：「歸與！

translations.literal: Therefore Confucius, when he was in Chen, said: "Let me go home!
translations.idiomatic: And so Confucius, while in the state of Chen, once said: "Let me go home!

sentences.zh: 歸與！

translations.literal: Let me go home!
translations.idiomatic: Let me go home!

sentences.zh: 吾黨之小子狂簡，斐然成章，不知所以裁之。」

translations.literal: The young men of my school are impetuous and careless; they have produced brilliant patterns, but do not know how to trim them."
translations.idiomatic: The young men back home are ambitious yet unrestrained; they have produced splendid writings, but do not yet know how to shape them."

sentences.zh: 夫齊魯之閒於文學，自古以來，其天性也。

translations.literal: As for the region between Qi and Lu and its devotion to literature and learning, from ancient times onward, this has been its innate character.
translations.idiomatic: The devotion to literature and learning in the lands between Qi and Lu has been part of their very nature since ancient times.

sentences.zh: 故漢興，然後諸儒始得修其經藝，講習大射鄉飲之禮。

translations.literal: Therefore, when the Han arose, the Confucian scholars were at last able to cultivate their classical arts and lecture on and practice the rites of the Great Archery Ceremony and the District Drinking Ceremony.
translations.idiomatic: Only after the founding of the Han were Confucian scholars at last able to resume their classical studies and practice the rites of the Great Archery Ceremony and the District Drinking Ceremony.

sentences.zh: 叔孫通作漢禮儀，因為太常，諸生弟子共定者，咸為選首，於是喟然嘆興於學。

translations.literal: Shusun Tong created the Han court rituals and was thereupon made Grand Master of Ceremonies; the students and disciples who had helped establish them were all selected for the highest appointments, and at this they sighed with admiration and a passion for learning was aroused.
translations.idiomatic: Shusun Tong devised the Han court rituals and was appointed Grand Master of Ceremonies. The students and disciples who had helped establish these rites were all awarded top appointments, inspiring a great surge of admiration and enthusiasm for learning.

sentences.zh: 然尚有干戈，平定四海，亦未暇遑庠序之事也。

translations.literal: However, there were still weapons of war, and in pacifying the realm, there was no leisure for the affairs of schools.
translations.idiomatic: Yet with warfare still raging as the empire was being pacified, there was no time to attend to the affairs of the academies.

sentences.zh: 孝惠、呂后時，公卿皆武力有功之臣。

translations.literal: During the time of Emperor Hui and Empress Dowager Lu, the high ministers were all men who had earned merit through military prowess.
translations.idiomatic: In the reigns of Emperor Hui and Empress Dowager Lu, the high ministers were all men who had won their rank through military achievement.

sentences.zh: 孝文時頗徵用，然孝文帝本好刑名之言。

translations.literal: During the time of Emperor Wen, scholars were somewhat recruited and employed, but Emperor Wen was fundamentally fond of the doctrines of punishment and names.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wen, scholars were recruited to some extent, but the emperor himself was primarily drawn to the Legalist doctrines of punishment and terminology.

sentences.zh: 及至孝景，不任儒者，而竇太后又好黃老之術，故諸博士具官待問，未有進者。

translations.literal: When it came to Emperor Jing, he did not employ Confucian scholars, and Empress Dowager Dou was furthermore fond of the arts of Huang-Lao; therefore the various erudites merely held office and awaited inquiries, with none being promoted.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Jing, Confucian scholars found no favor, and Empress Dowager Dou was herself devoted to the teachings of Huang-Lao. The erudites merely held their posts and awaited inquiries, with none advancing in rank.

sentences.zh: 及今上即位，趙綰、王臧之屬明儒學，而上亦鄉之，於是招方正賢良文學之士。

translations.literal: When the present emperor ascended the throne, men such as Zhao Wan and Wang Zang, who were well versed in Confucian learning, came to prominence, and the emperor also inclined toward it; thereupon he recruited men who were upright, worthy, and accomplished in letters.
translations.idiomatic: When the present emperor ascended the throne, men like Zhao Wan and Wang Zang, distinguished in Confucian learning, came to prominence, and the emperor himself was drawn to their tradition. He summoned men who were upright, worthy, and accomplished in the literary arts.

sentences.zh: 自是之后，言詩於魯則申培公，於齊則轅固生，於燕則韓太傅。

translations.literal: From this time onward, those who expounded the Odes included Master Shen Pei in Lu, Yuan Gu in Qi, and Grand Tutor Han in Yan.
translations.idiomatic: From that time on, the foremost exponents of the Odes were Master Shen Pei in Lu, Yuan Gu in Qi, and Grand Tutor Han in Yan.

sentences.zh: 言尚書自濟南伏生。

translations.literal: The transmission of the Documents came from Master Fu of Jinan.
translations.idiomatic: The tradition of the Documents was transmitted from Master Fu of Jinan.

sentences.zh: 言禮自魯高堂生。

translations.literal: The transmission of the Rites came from Master Gaotang of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: The tradition of the Rites was transmitted from Master Gaotang of Lu.

sentences.zh: 言易自菑川田生。

translations.literal: The transmission of the Changes came from Master Tian of Zichuan.
translations.idiomatic: The tradition of the Changes was transmitted from Master Tian of Zichuan.

sentences.zh: 言春秋於齊魯自胡毋生，於趙自董仲舒。

translations.literal: The transmission of the Spring and Autumn Annals in Qi and Lu came from Humu Sheng, and in Zhao from Dong Zhongshu.
translations.idiomatic: The tradition of the Spring and Autumn Annals was transmitted by Humu Sheng in the regions of Qi and Lu, and by Dong Zhongshu in Zhao.

sentences.zh: 及竇太后崩，武安侯田蚡為丞相，絀黃老、刑名百家之言，延文學儒者數百人，而公孫弘以春秋白衣為天子三公，封以平津侯。

translations.literal: When Empress Dowager Dou died, the Marquis of Wu'an, Tian Fen, became Chancellor. He rejected the teachings of Huang-Lao, Legalism, and the Hundred Schools, and recruited several hundred scholars of literature and Confucian learning. Gongsun Hong, through his mastery of the Spring and Autumn Annals, rose from commoner status to become one of the Three Excellencies of the Son of Heaven, and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Pingjin.
translations.idiomatic: After Empress Dowager Dou died, the Marquis of Wu'an, Tian Fen, became Chancellor. He dismissed the doctrines of Huang-Lao, Legalism, and the Hundred Schools, and recruited several hundred literary and Confucian scholars. Gongsun Hong, through his mastery of the Spring and Autumn Annals, rose from commoner to one of the Three Excellencies under the Son of Heaven, and was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Pingjin.

sentences.zh: 天下之學士靡然鄉風矣。

translations.literal: The scholars throughout the realm all turned toward this trend like grass bending before the wind.
translations.idiomatic: Scholars throughout the realm turned toward this new wind like grass bending in a breeze.

sentences.zh: 公孫弘為學官，悼道之郁滯，乃請曰：「丞相御史言：制曰『蓋聞導民以禮，風之以樂。

translations.literal: Gongsun Hong, as the official in charge of learning, lamented the stagnation of the Way, and submitted a memorial saying: "The Chancellor and the Imperial Secretary state: The imperial edict says, 'We have heard that the people should be guided by ritual and influenced by music.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Hong, as the official overseeing scholarship, lamented the stagnation of the Way and submitted a memorial: "The Chancellor and the Imperial Secretary state: The imperial edict reads, 'We have heard that the people should be guided through ritual and transformed through music.

sentences.zh: 婚姻者，居屋之大倫也。

translations.literal: Marriage is the great moral bond of the household.
translations.idiomatic: Marriage is the foremost bond of the household.

sentences.zh: 今禮廢樂崩，朕甚愍焉。

translations.literal: Now ritual has been abandoned and music has collapsed, and We are deeply saddened by this.
translations.idiomatic: Now that ritual has fallen into disuse and music has collapsed, We are deeply grieved.

sentences.zh: 故詳延天下方正博聞之士，咸登諸朝。

translations.literal: Therefore We have carefully invited from throughout the realm men who are upright and broadly learned, and brought them all to court.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore We have carefully summoned from throughout the realm men who are upright and broadly learned, and brought them all to court.

sentences.zh: 其令禮官勸學，講議洽聞興禮，以為天下先。

translations.literal: Let the ritual officials encourage learning, lecturing and discussing broadly what they have heard to revive the rites, so as to set an example for the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Let the ritual officials promote learning, and through broad discussion and inquiry revive the rites, so as to lead the realm by example.

sentences.zh: 太常議，與博士弟子，崇鄉里之化，以廣賢材焉』。

translations.literal: Let the Grand Master of Ceremonies deliberate with the erudites and their disciples, promoting moral transformation in the local communities, so as to broaden the pool of worthy talent.'
translations.idiomatic: Let the Grand Master of Ceremonies deliberate with the erudites and their disciples on promoting moral transformation in the local communities, thereby expanding the pool of worthy talent.'

sentences.zh: 謹與太常臧、博士平等議曰：聞三代之道，鄉里有教，夏曰校，殷曰序，周曰庠。

translations.literal: We have respectfully deliberated with Grand Master of Ceremonies Zang, Erudite Ping, and others, and state: We have heard that under the Way of the Three Dynasties, there was education in the local communities — the Xia called their schools xiao, the Shang called them xu, and the Zhou called them xiang.
translations.idiomatic: After respectful deliberation with the Grand Master of Ceremonies Zang, the Erudite Ping, and others, we state: We have heard that under the Way of the Three Dynasties, there were local schools — the Xia called them xiao, the Shang called them xu, and the Zhou called them xiang.

sentences.zh: 其勸善也，顯之朝廷；

translations.literal: To encourage the good, they honored them at court;
translations.idiomatic: Those who excelled in virtue were honored at court;

sentences.zh: 其懲惡也，加之刑罰。

translations.literal: to punish the wicked, they imposed penalties upon them.
translations.idiomatic: those who did evil were subject to punishment.

sentences.zh: 故教化之行也，建首善自京師始，由內及外。

translations.literal: Therefore in the conduct of moral transformation, the establishment of paramount virtue begins from the capital and extends from within outward.
translations.idiomatic: In carrying out moral transformation, the standard of virtue must be established first in the capital and then spread outward.

sentences.zh: 今陛下昭至德，開大明，配天地，本人倫，勸學修禮，崇化厲賢，以風四方，太平之原也。

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty has made manifest the utmost virtue, opened the way to great illumination, matched Heaven and Earth, established the foundation of human relationships, encouraged learning and cultivated ritual, exalted moral transformation and inspired the worthy — this is to influence the four quarters and is the wellspring of great peace.
translations.idiomatic: Now Your Majesty has made manifest the highest virtue, opened the way to great enlightenment, matched Heaven and Earth, and established the foundation of human relations. By encouraging learning, cultivating ritual, exalting moral transformation, and inspiring the worthy, you influence the four corners of the realm — this is the very wellspring of an era of great peace.

sentences.zh: 古者政教未洽，不備其禮，請因舊官而興焉。

translations.literal: In ancient times, when governance and education had not yet been fully harmonized and the rites were not yet complete, they relied upon the existing officials to bring about their revival.
translations.idiomatic: In ancient times, when governance and instruction had not yet been fully established and the rites were still incomplete, the existing officials were relied upon to bring about their revival.

sentences.zh: 為博士官置弟子五十人，復其身。

translations.literal: We request that fifty disciples be assigned to the office of the erudites, with their persons exempt from labor service.
translations.idiomatic: We request that fifty students be assigned to the office of the erudites, with each granted exemption from corvee labor.

sentences.zh: 太常擇民年十八已上，儀狀端正者，補博士弟子。

translations.literal: The Grand Master of Ceremonies shall select from the common people those who are eighteen years of age or above and of proper bearing and appearance, to fill the positions of erudite disciples.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Master of Ceremonies shall select from among the people those who are eighteen years of age or older and of proper bearing and appearance, to fill the positions of erudite students.

sentences.zh: 郡國縣道邑有好文學，敬長上，肅政教，順鄉里，出入不悖所聞者，令相長丞上屬所二千石，二千石謹察可者，當與計偕，詣太常，得受業如弟子。

translations.literal: In the commanderies, kingdoms, counties, districts, and towns, those who are fond of literature and learning, respectful toward their superiors, attentive to governance and education, compliant with their communities, and whose conduct does not violate what they have been taught, shall be reported by their magistrates, chiefs, and deputies to the office of the two-thousand-bushel administrator. The administrator shall carefully examine those who qualify, and they shall be sent along with the annual accounts to the Grand Master of Ceremonies, where they may receive instruction as disciples.
translations.idiomatic: In the commanderies, kingdoms, counties, districts, and towns, those who are devoted to learning, respectful toward their elders, attentive to governance and instruction, well regarded in their communities, and whose conduct accords with what they have been taught, shall be reported by the local magistrates and their deputies to the two-thousand-bushel administrator. The administrator shall carefully evaluate the qualified candidates, who shall then be sent to the capital with the annual accounts to study under the Grand Master of Ceremonies as students.

sentences.zh: 一歲皆輒試，能通一藝以上，補文學掌故缺；

translations.literal: Each year they shall all be examined, and those who can master one or more of the arts shall fill vacancies as literary scholars or keepers of precedents;
translations.idiomatic: Each year they shall be examined. Those who have mastered one or more of the classical arts shall fill vacancies as literary scholars or keepers of precedents;

sentences.zh: 其高弟可以為郎中者，太常籍奏。

translations.literal: the top students who are qualified to serve as Gentlemen of the Interior shall be recorded by the Grand Master of Ceremonies and reported to the throne.
translations.idiomatic: the highest-ranking students who qualify to serve as Gentlemen of the Interior shall be registered by the Grand Master of Ceremonies and reported to the throne.

sentences.zh: 即有秀才異等，輒以名聞。

translations.literal: If there are those of exceptional talent and outstanding caliber, their names shall be promptly made known.
translations.idiomatic: Should there be candidates of exceptional talent and outstanding ability, their names shall be promptly reported.

sentences.zh: 其不事學若下材及不能通一藝，輒罷之，而請諸不稱者罰。

translations.literal: Those who do not apply themselves to learning, or are of inferior ability, or cannot master a single art, shall be immediately dismissed, and we request that those who fail to meet the standard be punished.
translations.idiomatic: Those who do not apply themselves to their studies, who are of inferior ability, or who cannot master even a single art, shall be dismissed. We further request that those who fail to meet the standard be subject to punishment.

sentences.zh: 臣謹案詔書律令下者，明天人分際，通古今之義，文章爾雅，訓辭深厚，恩施甚美。

translations.literal: Your servant has carefully examined the edicts and statutory orders that have been promulgated: they clarify the distinctions between Heaven and humanity, penetrate the meaning of past and present, their writing is refined and elegant, their admonitory language is profound, and their beneficence is most admirable.
translations.idiomatic: Your servant has carefully examined the imperial edicts and statutory orders that have been promulgated: they clarify the boundary between Heaven and humanity, illuminate the significance of past and present, their prose is refined and elegant, their instructive language is profound, and their beneficence is most admirable.

sentences.zh: 小吏淺聞，不能究宣，無以明布諭下。

translations.literal: The petty officials, with their shallow understanding, are unable to fully explain and promulgate them, and have no means to clearly disseminate the message to those below.
translations.idiomatic: Yet the petty officials, with their shallow understanding, are unable to fully expound and promulgate these edicts, and thus cannot clearly convey the message to the people below.

sentences.zh: 治禮次治掌故，以文學禮義為官，遷留滯。

translations.literal: Those who have studied the rites and then studied precedents, and who hold office through literary learning and ritual propriety, have been stalled in their promotions.
translations.idiomatic: Those who have studied the rites and mastered precedents, and who serve in office by virtue of their literary learning and knowledge of ritual, have long been stalled in their advancement.

sentences.zh: 請選擇其秩比二百石以上，及吏百石通一藝以上，補左右內史、大行卒史；

translations.literal: We request that from among those with a salary rank equivalent to two hundred bushels or above, and officials of one hundred bushels who have mastered one or more arts, selections be made to fill vacancies as attendant clerks under the Left and Right Metropolitan Governors and the Grand Herald;
translations.idiomatic: We request that from among those with a salary rank of two hundred bushels or above, and officials of one hundred bushels who have mastered at least one classical art, selections be made to fill positions as attendant clerks under the Left and Right Metropolitan Governors and the Grand Herald;

sentences.zh: 比百石已下，補郡太守卒史：皆各二人，邊郡一人。

translations.literal: those ranked below one hundred bushels shall fill vacancies as attendant clerks under the commandery governors: two for each, and one for each border commandery.
translations.idiomatic: those ranked below one hundred bushels shall fill positions as attendant clerks under the commandery governors — two per commandery, and one for each border commandery.

sentences.zh: 先用誦多者，若不足，乃擇掌故補中二千石屬，文學掌故補郡屬，備員。

translations.literal: Priority shall be given to those who have recited the most; if these are insufficient, then keepers of precedents shall be selected to fill positions under the two-thousand-bushel officials, and literary scholars and keepers of precedents shall fill commandery positions, to complete the roster.
translations.idiomatic: Priority shall be given to those who have mastered the most texts. If these are insufficient, keepers of precedents shall be selected to fill posts under the two-thousand-bushel officials, and literary scholars and keepers of precedents shall fill commandery posts to complete the roster.

sentences.zh: 請著功令。

translations.literal: We request that this be recorded in the imperial statutes.
translations.idiomatic: We request that this be inscribed in the imperial statutes.

sentences.zh: 佗如律令。」

translations.literal: All else shall be in accordance with existing laws and ordinances."
translations.idiomatic: All other matters shall follow the existing laws and ordinances."

sentences.zh: 制曰：「可。」

translations.literal: The imperial edict said: "Approved."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor decreed: "Approved."

sentences.zh: 自此以來，則公卿大夫士吏斌斌多文學之士矣。

translations.literal: From this time onward, among the high ministers, grandees, scholars, and officials, there were abundantly many men of literary learning.
translations.idiomatic: From this time onward, the ranks of the high ministers, grandees, and officials were filled with men distinguished in literary learning.

sentences.zh: 申公者，魯人也。

translations.literal: Master Shen was a man of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Master Shen was a native of Lu.

sentences.zh: 高祖過魯，申公以弟子從師入見高祖于魯南宮。

translations.literal: When the High Ancestor passed through Lu, Master Shen, as a disciple, accompanied his teacher to have an audience with the High Ancestor at the Southern Palace of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: When the High Ancestor passed through Lu, Master Shen accompanied his teacher as a student to have an audience with the High Ancestor at the Southern Palace of Lu.

sentences.zh: 呂太后時，申公游學長安，與劉郢同師。

translations.literal: During the time of Empress Dowager Lu, Master Shen traveled to Chang'an to study, and he had the same teacher as Liu Ying.
translations.idiomatic: During the time of Empress Dowager Lu, Master Shen traveled to Chang'an to study and shared the same teacher as Liu Ying.

sentences.zh: 已而郢為楚王，令申公傅其太子戊。

translations.literal: Later, when Ying became King of Chu, he ordered Master Shen to tutor his heir apparent, Wu.
translations.idiomatic: In time, Liu Ying became King of Chu and appointed Master Shen as tutor to his heir apparent, Wu.

sentences.zh: 戊不好學，疾申公。

translations.literal: Wu did not like learning and resented Master Shen.
translations.idiomatic: Wu had no love of learning and came to resent Master Shen.

sentences.zh: 及王郢卒，戊立為楚王，胥靡申公。

translations.literal: When King Ying died and Wu was established as King of Chu, he had Master Shen punished as a convict laborer.
translations.idiomatic: When King Ying died and Wu ascended as King of Chu, he sentenced Master Shen to servitude as a convict laborer.

sentences.zh: 申公恥之，歸魯，退居家教，終身不出門，復謝絕賓客，獨王命召之乃往。

translations.literal: Master Shen was ashamed of this, returned to Lu, withdrew to teach at home, and for the rest of his life never went out. He further declined all guests and visitors, and would only go if summoned by the king's command.
translations.idiomatic: Humiliated, Master Shen returned to Lu and withdrew to teach from his home. For the rest of his life he never stepped outside his gate. He refused all visitors, and would venture forth only when summoned by royal command.

sentences.zh: 弟子自遠方至受業者百餘人。

translations.literal: Disciples who came from distant places to receive his instruction numbered more than a hundred.
translations.idiomatic: More than a hundred students came from distant places to study under him.

sentences.zh: 申公獨以詩經為訓以教，無傳（疑），疑者則闕不傳。

translations.literal: Master Shen taught solely through the explication of the Classic of Odes; he had no transmitted commentary, and where there was doubt, he left gaps and did not transmit.
translations.idiomatic: Master Shen taught solely through the explication of the Classic of Odes. He left no transmitted commentary, and where there was any doubt, he simply left a gap rather than pass on uncertain material.

sentences.zh: 蘭陵王臧既受詩，以事孝景帝為太子少傅，免去。

translations.literal: Wang Zang of Lanling, having received instruction in the Odes, served Emperor Jing as Junior Tutor to the Heir Apparent, but was dismissed and departed.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Zang of Lanling, having studied the Odes under Master Shen, served Emperor Jing as Junior Tutor to the Heir Apparent, but was later dismissed from the post.

sentences.zh: 今上初即位，臧乃上書宿衛上，累遷，一歲中為郎中令。

translations.literal: When the present emperor first ascended the throne, Zang submitted a memorial and was assigned to the palace guard; through successive promotions, within a single year he became the Commandant of the Gentlemen of the Interior.
translations.idiomatic: When the present emperor first ascended the throne, Wang Zang submitted a memorial and was assigned to the palace guard. Through successive promotions, he rose within a single year to become the Commandant of the Gentlemen of the Interior.

sentences.zh: 及代趙綰亦嘗受詩申公，綰為御史大夫。

translations.literal: Zhao Wan of Dai had also once studied the Odes under Master Shen; Wan became the Imperial Secretary.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Wan of Dai had also studied the Odes under Master Shen. He rose to become the Imperial Secretary.

sentences.zh: 綰、臧請天子，欲立明堂以朝諸侯，不能就其事，乃言師申公。

translations.literal: Wan and Zang petitioned the emperor, wishing to establish a Hall of Light for receiving audiences with the feudal lords; unable to bring the project to fruition, they then recommended their teacher, Master Shen.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Wan and Wang Zang petitioned the emperor to build a Hall of Light for receiving the feudal lords in audience. Unable to bring the project to fruition on their own, they recommended their teacher, Master Shen.

sentences.zh: 於是天子使使束帛加璧安車駟馬迎申公，弟子二人乘軺傳從。

translations.literal: Thereupon the emperor sent an envoy with bundles of silk and jade discs, along with a comfortable carriage drawn by four horses, to welcome Master Shen; two of his disciples rode in a light carriage and accompanied him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent an envoy bearing bundles of silk and jade discs, together with a comfortable carriage drawn by four horses, to welcome Master Shen. Two of his disciples rode in a light carriage to accompany him.

sentences.zh: 至，見天子。

translations.literal: When he arrived, he had an audience with the emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Upon his arrival, he was granted an audience with the emperor.

sentences.zh: 天子問治亂之事，申公時已八十餘，老，對曰：「為治者不在多言，顧力行何如耳。」

translations.literal: The emperor asked him about the principles of order and disorder. Master Shen was by then over eighty years old and aged; he replied: "Governing does not lie in many words, but only in how vigorously one acts."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked him about the principles of order and disorder. Master Shen, by then over eighty and frail with age, replied: "Good governance does not lie in many words — it lies only in the vigor of one's actions."

sentences.zh: 是時天子方好文詞，見申公對，默然。

translations.literal: At that time, the emperor was particularly fond of fine words and literary expression; upon hearing Master Shen's reply, he fell silent.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the emperor was particularly enamored of eloquent rhetoric. Hearing Master Shen's blunt reply, he fell silent.

sentences.zh: 然已招致，則以為太中大夫，舍魯邸，議明堂事。

translations.literal: Nevertheless, having already summoned him, he appointed him Grand Palace Grandee, lodged him at the Lu embassy, and had him deliberate on the Hall of Light project.
translations.idiomatic: Nevertheless, having already summoned him, the emperor appointed him Grand Palace Grandee, lodged him at the Lu embassy, and charged him with deliberating on the Hall of Light project.

sentences.zh: 太皇竇太后好老子言，不說儒術，得趙綰、王臧之過以讓上，上因廢明堂事，盡下趙綰、王臧吏，後皆自殺。

translations.literal: The Grand Empress Dowager Dou was fond of the teachings of Laozi and disliked Confucian learning. She found fault with Zhao Wan and Wang Zang and used their offenses to reproach the emperor. The emperor thereupon abandoned the Hall of Light project and handed Zhao Wan and Wang Zang over entirely to the judicial officials; both later took their own lives.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Empress Dowager Dou was devoted to the teachings of Laozi and had no use for Confucian learning. She seized upon the transgressions of Zhao Wan and Wang Zang to reproach the emperor. The emperor thereupon abandoned the Hall of Light project and turned Zhao Wan and Wang Zang over to the judicial officials. Both later took their own lives.

sentences.zh: 申公亦疾免以歸，數年卒。

translations.literal: Master Shen was also dismissed on account of illness and returned home; he died several years later.
translations.idiomatic: Master Shen, too, was dismissed on account of illness and returned home. He died a few years later.

sentences.zh: 弟子為博士者十餘人：孔安國至臨淮太守，周霸至膠西內史，夏寬至城陽內史，碭魯賜至東海太守，蘭陵繆生至長沙內史，徐偃為膠西中尉，鄒人闕門慶忌為膠東內史。

translations.literal: More than ten of his disciples became erudites: Kong Anguo rose to be Governor of Linhuai, Zhou Ba to be Metropolitan Governor of Jiaoxi, Xia Kuan to be Metropolitan Governor of Chengyang, Lu Ci of Dang to be Governor of Donghai, Master Miao of Lanling to be Metropolitan Governor of Changsha, Xu Yan to be Commandant of Jiaoxi, and Quemen Qingji of Zou to be Metropolitan Governor of Jiaodong.
translations.idiomatic: More than ten of his disciples became erudites: Kong Anguo rose to Governor of Linhuai, Zhou Ba to Metropolitan Governor of Jiaoxi, Xia Kuan to Metropolitan Governor of Chengyang, Lu Ci of Dang to Governor of Donghai, Master Miao of Lanling to Metropolitan Governor of Changsha, Xu Yan to Commandant of Jiaoxi, and Quemen Qingji of Zou to Metropolitan Governor of Jiaodong.

sentences.zh: 其治官民皆有廉節，稱其好學。

translations.literal: In governing officials and people, they all showed integrity and propriety, and were praised for their love of learning.
translations.idiomatic: In their governance of officials and people alike, they all demonstrated integrity and propriety, and were praised for their devotion to learning.

sentences.zh: 學官弟子行雖不備，而至於大夫、郎中、掌故以百數。

translations.literal: Although the conduct of the students of the official academy was not always impeccable, those who attained the rank of grandee, Gentleman of the Interior, or keeper of precedents numbered in the hundreds.
translations.idiomatic: Though the students of the official academy were not always impeccable in their conduct, those among them who rose to the rank of grandee, Gentleman of the Interior, or keeper of precedents numbered in the hundreds.

sentences.zh: 言詩雖殊，多本於申公。

translations.literal: Although the traditions of teaching the Odes differed, most traced their origins to Master Shen.
translations.idiomatic: Though the various traditions of the Odes differed from one another, most traced their origins to Master Shen.

sentences.zh: 清河王太傅轅固生者，齊人也。

translations.literal: Yuan Gusheng, Grand Tutor to the King of Qinghe, was a man of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Gusheng, who served as Grand Tutor to the King of Qinghe, was a native of Qi.

sentences.zh: 以治詩，孝景時為博士。

translations.literal: Through his mastery of the Odes, he became an erudite during the reign of Emperor Jing.
translations.idiomatic: Through his mastery of the Odes, he was appointed an erudite during the reign of Emperor Jing.

sentences.zh: 與黃生爭論景帝前。

translations.literal: He engaged in a debate with Master Huang before Emperor Jing.
translations.idiomatic: He once engaged in a debate with Master Huang in the presence of Emperor Jing.

sentences.zh: 黃生曰：「湯武非受命，乃弒也。」

translations.literal: Master Huang said: "Tang and Wu did not receive the Mandate of Heaven; what they did was regicide."
translations.idiomatic: Master Huang declared: "Tang and Wu did not receive the Mandate of Heaven — what they did was regicide."

sentences.zh: 轅固生曰：「不然。

translations.literal: Yuan Gusheng said: "That is not so.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Gusheng replied: "That is not so.

sentences.zh: 夫桀紂虐亂，天下之心皆歸湯武，湯武與天下之心而誅桀紂，桀紂之民不為之使而歸湯武，湯武不得已而立，非受命為何？」

translations.literal: Jie and Zhou were cruel and tyrannical, and the hearts of all under Heaven turned to Tang and Wu. Tang and Wu, in accord with the hearts of all under Heaven, punished Jie and Zhou. The people of Jie and Zhou refused to serve them and turned to Tang and Wu. Tang and Wu had no choice but to establish themselves as rulers. If this is not receiving the Mandate, then what is?"
translations.idiomatic: Jie and Zhou were cruel and tyrannical, and the hearts of all under Heaven turned to Tang and Wu. Tang and Wu, acting in accord with the will of the people, punished Jie and Zhou. The people under Jie and Zhou refused to serve their rulers and turned to Tang and Wu instead. Tang and Wu had no choice but to take the throne. If this is not receiving the Mandate of Heaven, then what is?"

sentences.zh: 黃生曰：「冠雖敝，必加於首；

translations.literal: Master Huang said: "A cap, though worn out, must still be placed upon the head;
translations.idiomatic: Master Huang replied: "A cap, however worn, must still be placed upon the head;

sentences.zh: 履雖新，必關於足。

translations.literal: shoes, though new, must still be placed upon the feet.
translations.idiomatic: shoes, however new, must still be worn on the feet.

sentences.zh: 何者，上下之分也。

translations.literal: Why? Because this is the distinction between high and low.
translations.idiomatic: Why? Because this is the distinction between what is above and what is below.

sentences.zh: 今桀紂雖失道，然君上也；

translations.literal: Now Jie and Zhou, though they lost the Way, were still the rulers above;
translations.idiomatic: Jie and Zhou, though they lost the Way, were still the sovereign above;

sentences.zh: 湯武雖聖，臣下也。

translations.literal: Tang and Wu, though sages, were still subjects below.
translations.idiomatic: Tang and Wu, though sages, were still subjects below.

sentences.zh: 夫主有失行，臣下不能正言匡過以尊天子，反因過而誅之，代立踐南面，非弒而何也？」

translations.literal: When the ruler has erred in conduct, if his subjects cannot speak forthrightly to correct his faults and thereby uphold the Son of Heaven, but instead seize upon his faults to execute him, take his place, and face south as ruler — if this is not regicide, then what is?"
translations.idiomatic: When the sovereign errs in conduct, if his subjects cannot speak forthrightly to correct his faults and thereby honor the Son of Heaven, but instead exploit those faults to execute him, take his place, and assume the throne — if that is not regicide, what is?"

sentences.zh: 轅固生曰：「必若所云，是高帝代秦即天子之位，非邪？」

translations.literal: Yuan Gusheng said: "If it must be as you say, then was the High Emperor wrong to replace Qin and assume the position of Son of Heaven?"
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Gusheng retorted: "If what you say must hold, then was the High Emperor wrong to replace Qin and take the throne?"

sentences.zh: 於是景帝曰：「食肉不食馬肝，不為不知味；

translations.literal: At this, Emperor Jing said: "One who eats meat but does not eat horse liver is not considered ignorant of flavors;
translations.idiomatic: At this, Emperor Jing intervened: "A man who eats meat but refuses horse liver is not thereby ignorant of flavor;

sentences.zh: 言學者無言湯武受命，不為愚。」

translations.literal: a scholar who does not speak of Tang and Wu receiving the Mandate is not thereby foolish."
translations.idiomatic: a scholar who declines to speak of Tang and Wu receiving the Mandate is not thereby a fool."

sentences.zh: 遂罷。

translations.literal: The debate was thereupon ended.
translations.idiomatic: The debate was brought to a close.

sentences.zh: 是後學者莫敢明受命放殺者。

translations.literal: From this time on, no scholar dared openly discuss the subject of receiving the Mandate and deposing and killing rulers.
translations.idiomatic: From then on, no scholar dared openly discuss the Mandate of Heaven or the deposing and slaying of rulers.

sentences.zh: 竇太后好老子書，召轅固生問老子書。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Dou was fond of the book of Laozi and summoned Yuan Gusheng to discuss it.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Dou was fond of the writings of Laozi and summoned Yuan Gusheng to discuss them.

sentences.zh: 固曰：「此是家人言耳。」

translations.literal: Yuan Gu said: "This is merely the talk of a commoner."
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Gu replied: "This is merely the talk of a commoner."

sentences.zh: 太后怒曰：「安得司空城旦書乎？」

translations.literal: The Empress Dowager was furious and said: "How is this any better than the writings of convicts sentenced to hard labor?"
translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager was enraged: "How would you like a taste of convict literature instead?"

sentences.zh: 乃使固入圈刺豕。

translations.literal: She then ordered Yuan Gu to enter the boar pen and fight a wild boar.
translations.idiomatic: She ordered Yuan Gu to enter the boar pen and fight a wild pig.

sentences.zh: 景帝知太后怒而固直言無罪，乃假固利兵，下圈刺豕，正中其心，一刺，豕應手而倒。

translations.literal: Emperor Jing, knowing the Empress Dowager was angry but that Yuan Gu had spoken forthrightly and committed no crime, provided Yuan Gu with a sharp weapon. He went down into the pen to fight the boar, struck it straight through the heart, and with a single thrust the boar fell at his hand.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing, knowing the Empress Dowager was enraged but that Yuan Gu had spoken forthrightly and was blameless, secretly provided him with a sharp weapon. Yuan Gu descended into the pen, struck the boar straight through the heart, and with a single thrust the beast dropped dead.

sentences.zh: 太后默然，無以復罪，罷之。

translations.literal: The Empress Dowager was silent, having no further grounds to punish him, and dismissed the matter.
translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager fell silent, having no further grounds to punish him, and let the matter drop.

sentences.zh: 居頃之，景帝以固為廉直，拜為清河王太傅。

translations.literal: Before long, Emperor Jing, regarding Yuan Gu as a man of integrity and honesty, appointed him Grand Tutor to the King of Qinghe.
translations.idiomatic: Before long, Emperor Jing, recognizing Yuan Gu as a man of integrity and forthright character, appointed him Grand Tutor to the King of Qinghe.

sentences.zh: 久之，病免。

translations.literal: After a long time, he was dismissed on account of illness.
translations.idiomatic: In time, he was relieved of his duties on account of illness.

sentences.zh: 今上初即位，復以賢良徵固。

translations.literal: When the present emperor first ascended the throne, Yuan Gu was again summoned on the recommendation that he was worthy and good.
translations.idiomatic: When the present emperor first ascended the throne, Yuan Gu was once again summoned as a worthy and virtuous scholar.

sentences.zh: 諸諛儒多疾毀固，曰「固老」，罷歸之。

translations.literal: The various sycophantic scholars mostly harbored jealousy and slandered Yuan Gu, saying "Yuan Gu is too old," and he was dismissed and sent home.
translations.idiomatic: The sycophantic scholars at court, harboring jealousy, slandered him, saying he was too old. He was dismissed and sent home.

sentences.zh: 時固已九十餘矣。

translations.literal: At that time, Yuan Gu was already over ninety years old.
translations.idiomatic: By that time, Yuan Gu was already over ninety years of age.

sentences.zh: 固之徵也，薛人公孫弘亦徵，側目而視固。
固曰：「公孫子，務正學以言，無曲學以阿世！」
自是之後，齊言詩皆本轅固生也。
諸齊人以詩顯貴，皆固之弟子也。
韓生者，燕人也。
孝文帝時為博士，景帝時為常山王太傅。
韓生推詩之意而為內外傳數萬言，其語頗與齊魯閒殊，然其歸一也。
淮南賁生受之。
自是之後，而燕趙閒言詩者由韓生。
韓生孫商為今上博士。
伏生者，濟南人也。
故為秦博士。
孝文帝時，欲求能治尚書者，天下無有，乃聞伏生能治，欲召之。
是時伏生年九十餘，老，不能行，於是乃詔太常使掌故晁錯往受之。
秦時焚書，伏生壁藏之。
其後兵大起，流亡，漢定，伏生求其書，亡數十篇，獨得二十九篇，即以教于齊魯之閒。
學者由是頗能言尚書，諸山東大師無不涉尚書以教矣。
伏生教濟南張生及歐陽生，歐陽生教千乘兒寬。
兒寬既通尚書，以文學應郡舉，詣博士受業，受業孔安國。
兒寬貧無資用，常為弟子都養，及時時閒行傭賃，以給衣食。
行常帶經，止息則誦習之。
以試第次，補廷尉史。
是時張湯方鄉學，以為奏讞掾，以古法議決疑大獄，而愛幸寬。
寬為人溫良，有廉智，自持，而善著書、書奏，敏於文，口不能發明也。
湯以為長者，數稱譽之。
及湯為御史大夫，以兒寬為掾，薦之天子。
天子見問，說之。
張湯死后六年，兒寬位至御史大夫。
九年而以官卒。
寬在三公位，以和良承意從容得久，然無有所匡諫；
於官，官屬易之，不為盡力。
張生亦為博士。
而伏生孫以治尚書徵，不能明也。
自此之後，魯周霸、孔安國，雒陽賈嘉，頗能言尚書事。
孔氏有古文尚書，而安國以今文讀之，因以起其家。
逸書得十餘篇，蓋尚書滋多於是矣。
諸學者多言禮，而魯高堂生最本。
禮固自孔子時而其經不具，及至秦焚書，書散亡益多，於今獨有士禮，高堂生能言之。
而魯徐生善為容。
孝文帝時，徐生以容為禮官大夫。
傳子至孫延、徐襄。
襄，其天姿善為容，不能通禮經；
延頗能，未善也。
襄以容為漢禮官大夫，至廣陵內史。
延及徐氏弟子公戶滿意、桓生、單次，皆嘗為漢禮官大夫。
而瑕丘蕭奮以禮為淮陽太守。
是後能言禮為容者，由徐氏焉。
自魯商瞿受易孔子，孔子卒，商瞿傳易，六世至齊人田何，字子莊，而漢興。
田何傳東武人王同子仲，子仲傳菑川人楊何。
何以易，元光元年徵，官至中大夫。
齊人即墨成以易至城陽相。
廣川人孟但以易為太子門大夫。
魯人周霸，莒人衡胡，臨菑人主父偃，皆以易至二千石。
然要言易者本於楊何之家。
董仲舒，廣川人也。
以治春秋，孝景時為博士。
下帷講誦，弟子傳以久次相受業，或莫見其面，蓋三年董仲舒不觀於舍園，其精如此。
進退容止，非禮不行，學士皆師尊之。
今上即位，為江都相。
以春秋災異之變推陰陽所以錯行，故求雨閉諸陽，縱諸陰，其止雨反是。
行之一國，未嘗不得所欲。
中廢為中大夫，居舍，著災異之記。
是時遼東高廟災，主父偃疾之，取其書奏之天子。
天子召諸生示其書，有刺譏。
董仲舒弟子呂步舒不知其師書，以為下愚。
於是下董仲舒吏，當死，詔赦之。
於是董仲舒竟不敢復言災異。
董仲舒為人廉直。
是時方外攘四夷，公孫弘治春秋不如董仲舒，而弘希世用事，位至公卿。
董仲舒以弘為從諛。
弘疾之，乃言上曰：「獨董仲舒可使相繆西王。」
膠西王素聞董仲舒有行，亦善待之。
董仲舒恐久獲罪，疾免居家。
至卒，終不治產業，以修學著書為事。
故漢興至于五世之閒，唯董仲舒名為明於春秋，其傳公羊氏也。
胡毋生，齊人也。
孝景時為博士，以老歸教授。
齊之言春秋者多受胡毋生，公孫弘亦頗受焉。
瑕丘江生為穀梁春秋。
自公孫弘得用，嘗集比其義，卒用董仲舒。
仲舒弟子遂者：蘭陵褚大，廣川殷忠，溫呂步舒。
褚大至梁相。
步舒至長史，持節使決淮南獄，於諸侯擅專斷，不報，以春秋之義正之，天子皆以為是。
弟子通者，至於命大夫；
為郎、謁者、掌故者以百數。
而董仲舒子及孫皆以學至大官。
